steps.title: Find tangent at angle Y
steps.content:
The altitude $WX$ forms right triangle $XWY$. Relative to angle $Y$, the opposite side is $WX=350$ and the adjacent side is $YW=840$, so:
$$\tan Y=\frac{350}{840}=\frac{5}{12}$$

steps.title: Use complementary angles
steps.content: In right triangle $XYZ$, angles $Y$ and $Z$ are complementary. Therefore, their tangent values are reciprocals: $$\tan Z=\frac{1}{\tan Y}=\frac{1}{\frac{5}{12}}=\frac{12}{5}$$ The answer is A.
